Module com.trifasico.x50zo
Package com.trifasico.x50zo.exceptions

Class InvalidPlayException

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Throwable
java.lang.Exception
com.trifasico.x50zo.exceptions.InvalidPlayException
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class InvalidPlayException extends Exception
Checked exception thrown when a player attempts to play a card that would cause the table sum to exceed 50, violating the principal game rule.

This is a checked exception because the caller (typically the turn manager or the UI controller) is expected to handle it explicitly — for example, by prompting the human player to choose a different card, or by triggering the elimination flow for a machine player.

Example usage:


 if (currentSum + card.getValue() > 50) {
     throw new InvalidPlayException(card, currentSum);
 }
Version:
1.0
Author:
Yostin Ramirez, Lesly Zapata, Joseph Terreros
See Also:

      public InvalidPlayException(Card card, int currentSum)
      Constructs an InvalidPlayException for the given card and current sum.
      Parameters:
      card - the card the player tried to play
      currentSum - the table sum at the time of the attempted play

      public Card getCard()
      Returns the card that caused the violation.
      Returns:
      the illegally played Card

    • getCurrentSum

      public int getCurrentSum()
      Returns the table sum at the time the exception was thrown.
      Returns:
      the current table sum
